top_themes.headline: Anthropic releases Fable 5; capability jump, prices double, guardrails tighten
top_themes.detail: Anthropic publicly released Claude Fable 5, a Mythos-class model that materially outperforms prior models on complex, long-duration tasks (e.g., Stripe migrating a 50M-line codebase in a day). Pricing is 2x Opus ($10/$50 per million tokens) with usage-credit requirements after 14 days, prompting 'the subsidy era is over' commentary — a signal that frontier labs will push pricing up ahead of Anthropic's IPO. New guardrails block cybersecurity/biology queries and explicitly restrict use for rival frontier LLM development. Read-through: continued strong AI demand and improving lab unit economics support the AI infrastructure capex complex.

top_themes.headline: Rates and Iran keep pressuring equities; chips hit hardest
top_themes.detail: S&P 500 closed down 1.6% Wednesday with chip stocks tumbling again, following Friday's 4.8% Nasdaq 100 drop after a hot jobs report reignited inflation/rate-hike fears. Equities have become unusually sensitive to 10-year yield moves into the May CPI print (consensus 4.2% y/y headline); Morgan Stanley's Wilson draws a 2021 parallel with a Warsh Fed leaning accommodative. Fresh US-Iran strikes pushed oil higher and weighed on futures. Direct risk to the portfolio's semis and AI-beta names.

top_themes.headline: Creative financing scales AI chip deals: Broadcom, Google, private credit
top_themes.detail: Broadcom is securing financing from Apollo and Blackstone for projects tied to its Anthropic and OpenAI chip deals, while Google is backstopping payments on Anthropic's $35B chip lease across five data centers. The circular, credit-enhanced structure of AI capex is deepening — positive near term for custom silicon and datacenter suppliers, but worth monitoring for systemic interdependence risk.

top_themes.headline: Datacenter leasing on track for ~30GW in 2026; capex breadth widens
top_themes.detail: SemiAnalysis (from Datacloud Cannes) sees Q2 leasing matching giant Q4/Q1 levels, with credible developers planning 5GW+ and even 20GW Texas sites, Anthropic on an 80GW roadmap, Microsoft leasing ~1GW in the US in Q2 plus a 2GW Europe region for OpenAI/Anthropic, and Meta actively leasing GWs (Applied Digital, Vantage Millersport). Single source, but highly material for portfolio power/cooling/electrical names (GEV, VRT, ETN, PWR, EME, CEG, VST) and networking/compute suppliers. Note Google moving away from turnkey leases toward powered land/shells, pressuring developer economics (Crusoe out of Wyoming).

top_themes.headline: SpaceX IPO this week; Starlink India approval stalls over Iran war
top_themes.detail: The SpaceX IPO prices later this week with index funds potentially absorbing ~30% of float, raising concerns about market plumbing and feedback loops. Separately, Starlink's India regulatory approval has stalled on security grounds tied to the Iran conflict, a pre-IPO headline risk. SpaceX also detailed its AI1 orbital compute satellite and has filed for up to one million orbital datacenter satellites.

positions.name: Oracle Corp
positions.notes: Shares fell ~6% in extended trading after reporting higher-than-expected datacenter spending; capex intensity remains the market's key concern into the print.

positions.name: Broadcom Inc
positions.notes: Reported to be securing financing from Apollo and Blackstone for projects tied to its Anthropic and OpenAI custom chip deals — supports the durability of its AI ASIC pipeline while shifting funding risk to private credit.

positions.name: Alphabet Inc
positions.notes: Backstopping Anthropic's $35B chip lease payments at five datacenters. SemiAnalysis reports Google is likely going direct with Tallgrass on the Wyoming site (Crusoe out), self-building datacenters and favoring powered land/shells over turnkey leases; also launched Gemini 3.5 Live Translate.

positions.name: Microsoft Corp
positions.notes: Leased nearly 1GW in the US in Q2 (on par with Q1) and is building a 2GW European region driven by OpenAI and Anthropic demand, ~1GW of which was signed in Q2.

positions.name: Meta Platforms Inc
positions.notes: Actively leasing GWs of capacity; likely the customer behind 810MW of Applied Digital deals and the 900MW Vantage Millersport site, expanding its Prometheus cluster to 3GW.

positions.name: Apollo Global Management Inc
positions.notes: Alongside Blackstone, providing financing for Broadcom projects tied to Anthropic and OpenAI chip deals — further evidence of private credit's growing role in AI infrastructure.

positions.name: Blackstone Inc
positions.notes: Co-financing Broadcom's Anthropic/OpenAI-linked chip projects with Apollo.

one_liner: Anthropic's pricier, more capable Fable 5 reinforces the AI capex supercycle — now increasingly funded by Google backstops and private credit via Broadcom — even as hot inflation data, Iran strikes, and Oracle's capex-driven after-hours drop keep rate-sensitive chip and AI names under pressure into CPI.
